purpose: GPU-only vs Hybrid (capacity + cpu-first) on Qwen2.5-1.5B 500 reqs in/out 128. NOTE: VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE=1.
host: H100 80GB x4 + Xeon Platinum 8480+ (96 vCPU KVM, 1 NUMA, 944 GB RAM)
router_stats_observed: first observed: GPU=4.4 tok/s (354 reqs), CPU=2.7 tok/s (2 reqs)
later: GPU=27.5 tok/s (499 reqs), CPU=2.7 tok/s (2 reqs)

key_findings.performance_regression: Hybrid wall is 7.6x of GPU-only (106.66 s vs 13.96 s) and output_throughput drops to ~7.4% of GPU-only.
key_findings.primary_cause_hypothesis: VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE=1 + VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE_EVERY=1 produces a per-call log line on the API server side AND the CPU engine, plus dispatch/router/CPU-ATTN markers. With 500 reqs × 128 output tokens, this becomes thousands of log lines per second. Stdout I/O lock contention serialises through the API server's main thread, which is the same thread that ships responses to clients. GPU-only does not exercise hybrid_core / cpu_attn paths, so it is unaffected.
key_findings.secondary: CPU lane took only 2/501 reqs (capacity router default behavior with cpu-first when slot stays full). Mean GPU TPOT bloated from 22.4 ms (GPU-only) to 60.1 ms (hybrid) — direct evidence that the slowdown is in the hosting process, not the GPU executor.
key_findings.abort_slot_leak: not reproduced — 500/500 success, in_flight=1/1 stable, no stuck slot.
key_findings.cpu_attention_path: ipex=7112 / custom_avx=0 / sdpa=0 — IPEX path consistent.

implications.trace_overhead: VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE must be 0 (or VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE_EVERY must be large) for any production-quality benchmark. Trace is only safe for small smoke runs or for reading first-N marker emissions.
next_actions: Re-run the same env with VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE=0 to measure the true capacity + cpu-first performance on 1.5B in=out=128.
Compare against the throughput-adaptive 500-prompt 1.5B (in/out=512) experiment that ran trace-off and matched gpu-only wall.
